summary: A noisy, heavily‑processed soundboard recording that many deem poorly reduced, yet the 1985 performance shines with an insanely good “High Time” and high‑energy moments.
review: Listeners are divided: the show is remembered for its raw power – especially a blistering “High Time” – and the electric Coliseum atmosphere, but the source suffers from aggressive noise reduction that makes it sound harsh and sometimes out of tune. While some fans enjoy the lo‑fi character, most agree the recording quality drags down an otherwise solid 1985 gig.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews are split between high praise for the performance and strong criticism of the noise‑reduced soundboard, resulting in an overall middle rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Aggressive noise reduction described as the worst ever, causing harshness, loss of crispness, and occasional out‑of‑tune feel; some listeners note a “crispy ’85 board” vibe but most find it muddy and unsuitable for circulation.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans recall an energetic, loud show with memorable moments (e.g., fireworks, audience vote), though several note sloppy song transitions and uneven pacing between sets.
